variants.subject: Congress just increased affordable housing funding to $38.4B — here's why it matters
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

{{opener}}

I wanted to reach out because something big just happened in affordable housing that most investors haven't caught yet.

Congress rejected $33B in proposed HUD cuts and increased Section 8 funding to $38.4 billion — the largest increase in years. Total HUD budget: $77.3B. Meanwhile, there are 7.2 million families on waitlists for affordable housing.

That's the environment we operate in at Granite Park Capital. Our Fund I delivered 179% return on equity in 2 years — every dollar backed by government-contracted rent.

We're now raising Fund II ($50M target) using the same playbook:

• Government-guaranteed Section 8 rental income
• Quarterly distributions
• 4th-generation operator, 17,000+ units, ~$2B portfolio
• Tax advantages: LIHTC credits, cost segregation, accelerated depreciation
• $250K minimum, accredited investors only

15 minutes — happy to walk you through how this fits {{companyName}}'s allocation strategy.
Book a quick call

Best,

variants.subject: Quick tax question for {{companyName}}
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

One thing I didn't mention in my last note — the tax structure on our fund is unusual.

We generate Low Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC) that provide dollar-for-dollar offsets against federal tax liability. Not deductions — actual credits. For someone in the 37% bracket, this is a massive return enhancer on top of the cash distributions.

That's layered on top of cost segregation, accelerated depreciation, and tax-free cash-out refinances. Fund I investors saw 179% return on equity in 2 years — and kept more of it because of the tax structure.

{{pain_point}} — if that resonates, I'd love to show you how the numbers work for your situation specifically.

Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: Why Congress is funding our business model
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I keep getting asked: "With all the political noise, is affordable housing still safe?"

The answer is yes — and the numbers just got significantly better:

Federal Funding (FY2026):
• Section 8 budget: $38.4B (+$2.4B increase)
• Total HUD budget: $77.3B (+$7.2B over prior year)
• 60,000 new Housing Choice Vouchers distributed
• Congress rejected $33B in proposed cuts and increased funding instead

LIHTC Expansion (now permanent law):
• Bond financing threshold cut from 50% to 25% — massive unlock for new deals
• Projected to create 1.22 million additional affordable homes over the next decade

The demand:
• 7.2 million affordable rental home shortage nationwide
• Section 8 waitlists run 4-8+ years in major cities

This is why our Fund I delivered 179% ROE in 2 years — and why Fund II is positioned for even more favorable conditions.

15 minutes — I'll walk you through exactly how we capture this:
Book a quick call

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: 179% ROE in 2 years — here's how Fund I did it
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

I wanted to share something concrete rather than just another pitch.

Our Fund I just hit a milestone: 179% return on equity in 2 years. Fully subscribed at $50M. Every projection hit.

How we did it:

1. Acquired stabilized multifamily communities with government Section 8 contracts
2. Government-guaranteed rent — not dependent on market conditions
3. Cost segregation studies to accelerate depreciation for tax savings
4. Cash-out refinances (tax-free) to return capital while retaining the asset
5. Our parent company Apartment Corp (17,000+ units, ~$2B portfolio) operates every property in-house

Fund II uses the same playbook. Past 30% subscribed on the $50M raise.

If you allocate to alternatives at {{companyName}}, this might be worth a conversation.

Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: What makes Granite Park different from every other RE fund
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

There are a lot of real estate funds out there. Here's why ours is different:

1. Government-backed income. Section 8 contracts mean the federal government pays the rent. Not tenants, not the market — the government. That's recession-proof income.

2. Dollar-for-dollar tax credits. LIHTC credits offset your federal tax liability 1:1. For a high-income investor, this alone can represent a significant portion of the return.

3. 4th-generation operator. Our parent company Apartment Corp manages 17,000+ units (~$2B portfolio). This isn't a first-time fund manager — it's a multi-generational real estate family.

4. Fund I proved it. 179% ROE in 2 years. Fully subscribed at $50M. Every quarter, every projection — delivered.

5. Skin in the game. GP commits significant capital alongside every LP.

Class B/C affordable housing is outperforming Class A on rent growth, occupancy, and cap rates right now. Happy to show you the data.

Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: 7.2 million families are waiting — that's our opportunity
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

There are 7.2 million families on waitlists for affordable housing in the US. Only 35 affordable homes are available for every 100 extremely low-income households. Waitlists in major metros run 4-8+ years.

Meanwhile, Congress just increased Section 8 funding to $38.4B and permanently expanded LIHTC credits. The government is spending record amounts trying to close this gap — and it's barely making a dent.

That's the environment we invest in at Granite Park Capital. Unlimited demand, government-guaranteed rent, and a 4th-generation operator with 17,000+ units who knows how to execute.

Fund I: 179% ROE in 2 years. Fund II: same strategy, larger scale.

{{pain_point}} — if that's on your radar, I think this is worth a quick conversation.

Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: How our LPs keep more of what they make
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

One thing that surprises most investors about our fund — the tax advantages are built into the structure from day one:

LIHTC Tax Credits
Dollar-for-dollar offsets against your federal tax liability. Not deductions — actual credits. For someone in the 37% bracket, this is a massive return enhancer.

Cost Segregation + Accelerated Depreciation
We run cost seg studies on every acquisition to accelerate depreciation — creating significant paper losses that offset taxable income in the early years.

Cash-Out Refinance = Tax-Free Access
As properties appreciate, we use cash-out refinances to return capital. Refinance proceeds are not a taxable event — you access gains without triggering taxes.

1031 Exchange Friendly
Sitting on gains from another property? We're structured to accept 1031 exchanges into the fund.

Fund I delivered 179% ROE in 2 years using this exact strategy — and investors kept more of it because of the tax structure.

Want to see how the numbers work for your situation?
Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital

variants.subject: The full return stack (it's not just the 7% pref)
variants.body:
Hi {{firstName}},

Most people hear "7% preferred return" and think that's the whole story. It's not — it's just the floor.

Here's the full return stack for Fund II:

1. 7% preferred return — quarterly, from government Section 8 income
2. Appreciation — on asset repositioning
3. LIHTC tax credits — dollar-for-dollar federal offsets (not deductions)
4. Cost segregation — accelerated depreciation for early-year tax savings
5. Cash-out refinances — tax-free access to gains
6. 1031 exchange eligible — defer and redeploy existing gains

That's how Fund I delivered 179% return on equity in 2 years. The income is the base. The tax structure is what supercharges the total return.

If you or your clients are looking for tax-efficient alternatives exposure, I'd love to show you the full picture.

Book 15 minutes

Best,
Colby Watkins
Fund Manager | Granite Park Capital
